sections.title: What is a Set?
sections.description: A set is a number of things or people that we group together. A set can also be empty.

questions.prompt: What is a set?
options: A number of things grouped together
questions.correct: A number of things grouped together
questions.hint: A set is a group — like a set of books or a set of dogs

questions.prompt: A set that has nothing in it is called a ___ set.
questions.hint: If there is nothing inside, the set is empty

questions.prompt: Which of these is an example of a set?
questions.hint: A set is a COLLECTION of things — not just one thing or a number

questions.prompt: An empty set has ___ members.
questions.hint: Empty means nothing is inside — so zero members!

questions.prompt: A set can have ___ members.
questions.hint: A set can be empty (zero) or have any number of members

sections.title: Identifying Full and Empty Sets
sections.description: A full set has members inside it. An empty set has nothing in it.

questions.prompt: A box with nothing inside is an ___ set.
questions.hint: If there is nothing inside, the set is empty — it has zero members

questions.prompt: A basket with 5 apples is a ___ set.
questions.hint: If there are things inside, it is a full set

questions.prompt: If a box is empty, how many members does the set have?
questions.hint: Empty means nothing — zero members in the set

questions.prompt: A set of dogs with 6 dogs is a ___ set.
questions.hint: 6 dogs means something IS inside — so it is a full set

questions.prompt: Which shows an EMPTY set?
questions.hint: Empty means nothing is inside — look for the one with nothing in it

sections.title: How Many? Counting Set Members
sections.description: Count how many members are in a set, then write the number name.

questions.prompt: The word name for the number 15 is ___.
questions.hint: 15 = fifteen — fif-TEEN

questions.prompt: The numeral for 'nine' is ___.
questions.hint: Nine is the number that comes after eight — it is written as 9

questions.prompt: The word name for 20 is ___.
questions.hint: 20 = twenty — twen-TY

questions.prompt: The numeral for 'eleven' is ___.
questions.hint: Eleven comes after ten — it is written as 11

questions.prompt: The word name for 14 is ___.
questions.hint: 14 = fourteen — four-TEEN

questions.prompt: The numeral for 'seventeen' is ___.
questions.hint: Seventeen = 10 + 7 = 17

questions.prompt: A set has 8 corns. The word name for 8 is ___.
questions.hint: 8 = eight — just one syllable!

questions.prompt: The numeral for 'sixteen' is ___.
questions.hint: Sixteen = 10 + 6 = 16

sections.title: Equivalent Sets
sections.description: Sets that have the same number of members are called equivalent sets.

questions.prompt: Two sets that have the SAME number of members are called ___ sets.
questions.hint: Equivalent means equal in number — the same amount of members

questions.prompt: Set A has 4 birds. Set B has 4 caps. These sets are ___.
questions.hint: Both sets have 4 members — same number means equivalent!

questions.prompt: A set with 5 circles and a set with 5 triangles are ___.
questions.hint: Both sets have 5 members — equivalent sets!

questions.prompt: If Set A has 7 members and Set B has 9 members, are they equivalent?
questions.hint: 7 and 9 are different numbers — so the sets are NOT equivalent

questions.prompt: Which pair of sets is EQUIVALENT?
options: A set of 6 and a set of 8
A set of 10 and a set of 10
A set of 3 and a set of 5
A set of 12 and a set of 14
questions.correct: A set of 10 and a set of 10
questions.hint: Equivalent means SAME number — look for the pair with equal members

sections.title: Comparing Sets
sections.description: We compare sets to find which has more, which has less, or if they are equal.

questions.prompt: Set A has 6 cakes. Set B has 4 cakes. Which set has MORE?
questions.hint: 6 is more than 4 — so Set A has more cakes

questions.prompt: Set A has 14 bananas. Set B has 11 bananas. Which set has LESS?
questions.hint: 11 is less than 14 — so Set B has fewer bananas

questions.prompt: A set of 18 planes and a set of 16 planes. Which set has FEWER members?
questions.hint: 16 is less than 18 — so the set with 16 has fewer members

questions.prompt: Which set has MORE — a set of 12 butterflies or a set of 14 butterflies?
questions.hint: 14 is greater than 12 — so the set of 14 has more

questions.prompt: A set of 20 balls and a set of 14 books. Which has MORE members?
questions.hint: 20 is more than 14 — the set of balls has more members

sections.title: More Than, Less Than, Equal To
sections.description: We use the symbols > (greater than), < (less than), and = (equal to) to compare numbers.

questions.prompt: The symbol for 'greater than' is ___.
questions.hint: The 'greater than' symbol > points to the right and opens to the bigger number

questions.prompt: The symbol for 'less than' is ___.
questions.hint: The 'less than' symbol < points to the left and opens to the bigger number

questions.prompt: 8 is ___ than 3.
questions.hint: 8 > 3 — eight is greater than three

questions.prompt: 7 is ___ than 12.
questions.hint: 7 < 12 — seven is less than twelve

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this sentence true? 8 ___ 3
questions.hint: 8 is greater than 3, so we write 8 > 3

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this sentence true? 7 ___ 12
questions.hint: 7 is less than 12, so we write 7 < 12

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this sentence true? 15 ___ 15
questions.hint: 15 is equal to 15 — both sides are the same!

questions.prompt: Which makes the sentence true? 14 = ___
questions.hint: Equal means SAME — 14 is equal to 14

questions.prompt: Which statement is TRUE?
questions.hint: 8 is equal to 8 — any number is always equal to itself

questions.prompt: 12 is equal to 10 + ___.
